rationale.root_cause: It did not pinpoint the bug at line 11, did not use the exact term "global_buffer_overflow," and did not explicitly state that attacker-controlled argv data can exceed the buffer. Although it mentioned strcpy on argv[1], the rubric-required specifics were missing.
rationale.local_fix: It proposed strncpy with explicit NUL termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting oversized input in addition to bounded copying.
rationale.global_fix: It recommended eliminating unsafe string functions in favor of bounded APIs (e.g., strncpy/strlcpy/snprintf), which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path. This aligns with the stated root-cause fix options.
